AQA.name: Paper 1
AQA.title: Energy, Electricity, Particle model of matter, Atomic structure

Edexcel.name: Paper 1
Edexcel.title: Key concepts of physics, Motion, Forces, Conservation of energy, Waves, Radiation

Edexcel.name: Paper 2
Edexcel.title: Key concepts of physics, Motion, Forces, Conservation of energy, Waves, Radiation, Electromagnetism, Particle model

OCR.name: Paper 1
OCR.title: Matter, Forces, Energy, Waves, Electromagnetism, Radioactivity, Particle model, Electricity

OCR.name: Paper 2
OCR.title: Matter, Forces, Energy, Waves, Electromagnetism, Radioactivity, Particle model, Electricity

assessmentObjectives.description: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of scientific ideas, techniques and procedures
Apply knowledge and understanding of scientific ideas, techniques and procedures
Analyse information and ideas to interpret and evaluate scientific ideas and processes

topics.name: Energy Stores and Transfers
topics.description: Energy stores, systems and energy transfers including kinetic, gravitational potential, elastic potential, thermal, chemical and nuclear

topics.name: Conservation and Efficiency
topics.description: Conservation of energy, efficiency calculations and improving efficiency

topics.name: National and Global Energy
topics.description: Energy resources, renewable and non-renewable energy sources

topics.name: Specific Heat Capacity
topics.description: Specific heat capacity, SHC equation and required practical

topics.name: Specific Latent Heat
topics.description: Specific latent heat of fusion and vaporisation, heating and cooling curves

topics.name: Circuit Basics
topics.description: Standard circuit symbols, current, potential difference and resistance

topics.name: Series and Parallel Circuits
topics.description: Rules for current, voltage and resistance in series and parallel circuits

topics.name: Electrical Power and Energy
topics.description: Power, energy transfers and the National Grid

topics.name: Static Electricity
topics.description: Static charge, electric fields and their effects

topics.name: Density and States of Matter
topics.description: The particle model, density calculations and changes of state

topics.name: Particle Motion and Pressure
topics.description: Particle motion in gases, gas pressure and temperature relationships

topics.name: Nuclear Model and Isotopes
topics.description: Development of the nuclear model and isotopes

topics.name: Nuclear Radiation and Hazards
topics.description: Types of nuclear radiation, half-life and hazards of radiation

topics.name: Nuclear Fission and Fusion
topics.description: Nuclear fission, fusion and nuclear power

topics.name: Scalar and Vector Quantities
topics.description: Scalars, vectors and forces including weight and mass

topics.name: Motion and Acceleration
topics.description: Distance, displacement, speed, velocity and acceleration, motion graphs

topics.name: Newton's Laws and Momentum
topics.description: Newton's three laws of motion, inertia and momentum

topics.name: Stopping Distances
topics.description: Braking distance, thinking distance and factors affecting stopping distance

topics.name: Forces and Elasticity
topics.description: Hooke's law, springs and elastic deformation

topics.name: Pressure
topics.description: Pressure in fluids and atmospheric pressure

topics.name: Moments and Centre of Mass
topics.description: Moments, levers, gears and centre of mass

topics.name: Wave Properties
topics.description: Transverse and longitudinal waves, wave speed calculations and the ripple tank practical

topics.name: Electromagnetic Spectrum
topics.description: The EM spectrum, properties and uses of EM waves

topics.name: Sound and Ultrasound
topics.description: Sound waves, ultrasound and their applications

topics.name: Light and Optics
topics.description: Reflection, refraction, Snell's law and lenses

topics.name: Black Body Radiation
topics.description: Black body radiation and Earth's temperature and radiation balance

topics.name: Magnets and Magnetic Fields
topics.description: Permanent and induced magnets, magnetic fields and the Earth's magnetic field

topics.name: Electromagnets and the Motor Effect
topics.description: Electromagnets, the motor effect and Fleming's left-hand rule

topics.name: Electromagnetic Induction
topics.description: The generator effect, transformers and the National Grid

topics.name: Space Physics
topics.description: The Solar System, orbits, red-shift and the Big Bang theory

notes.tripleVsCombined: Triple Physics includes all Combined Science Physics content in greater depth, plus additional topics. Topics unique to Triple or significantly expanded include: specific latent heat, particle motion and pressure in more detail, moments and centre of mass, light and optics (Snell's law, lenses), black body radiation, and space physics (orbits, red-shift, Big Bang, life cycle of stars).
notes.requiredPracticals: AQA has 10 required practicals: SHC, thermal insulation, I-V characteristics, resistors in series/parallel, density, SHC of water, radiation absorption, force and extension, acceleration, waves in a ripple tank.
notes.mathsSkills: Physics requires the most maths of all sciences: rearranging equations, using standard form, significant figures, calculating gradients and areas under graphs, using tangents to curves, converting units, vector addition, resolving forces, using pro-ratio calculations.
notes.workingScientifically: All exam boards assess Working Scientifically: experimental design, variables, risk assessment, data analysis, evaluating methods, drawing conclusions, identifying sources of error.
